title: Parsonsia subuligera
taxonomy: Parsonsia subuligera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to Australia, where it is found in tropical and subtropical regions. Parsonsia subuligera is a member of the genus Parsonsia, which consists of around 25 species of flowering plants. The plant is also known as a part of the dogbane family due to its similarity in appearance to other plants in this family. Parsonsia subuligera is further classified as a species within the larger kingdom Plantae.

intro: Parsonsia subuligera, commonly known as the 'Pigeon Wood', is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Pacific Island cultures, to treat various health issues, including fever reduction and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
